channel.title: Daily Briefing
channel.description: A daily briefing on weather, tech, health, business, theology, philosophy, and clear thinking.

item.title: Daily Briefing — 2026-03-20
item.description:
Weather

The atmospheric river that has battered the Fraser Valley all week is finally losing its grip. Environment Canada still has a rainfall warning in effect for the central Fraser Valley, with another 10–20 mm expected before showers taper near midday. High near 11°C with southwest gusts to 50 km/h; overnight low around 4°C. The FVRD declared a state of emergency yesterday for Electoral Area E in the Chilliwack River Valley, and roughly 30 properties along the Chilliwack River remain under evacuation alert. A mudslide in Coquitlam forced a couple to be airlifted on Thursday. Saturday: 10°C with a 40% chance of showers. Sunday drier at 11°C. Trails saturated through the weekend.

Tech

Claude Code

Two releases in two days. Claude Code v2.1.81 shipped today with a --bare flag for scripted -p calls — skips hooks, LSP, plugin sync, and skill directory walks. Also: --channels permission relay, OAuth token refresh fixes, and MCP tool call output that collapses into a single line. Yesterday's v2.1.80 added rate_limits for statusline scripts, inline plugin declarations via source: 'settings', effort frontmatter for skills, and fixed --resume dropping parallel tool results.

Claude Platform

Anthropic published "What 81,000 People Want from AI," a qualitative study spanning 159 countries — the largest of its kind. Sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia substantially more optimistic than the West; independent workers reported three times the rate of economic empowerment. The Pentagon filed new declarations arguing Anthropic's employment of foreign nationals poses security risks, as part of its motion to dismiss the lawsuit. Jack Clark briefed House Homeland Security behind closed doors. Hearing on temporary relief set for March 24.

Competitors

OpenAI released GPT-5.4 Mini and Nano on March 17, now rolling out to ChatGPT Free and Go users. GPT-5.4 Mini approaches full GPT-5.4 on SWE-Bench Pro while running 2× faster. Gemini got a Workspace update — contextual drafting in Docs, AI spreadsheets in Sheets, deck generation in Slides, and cross-app intelligence.

Apple

Tim Cook: "best launch week ever for first-time Mac customers." All eight Neo configurations sold out online — delivery estimates pushed to April 6–13. New iPhone Fold patents show iPad-like multitasking layout.

General Interest

Super Micro Computer plunged 33% after co-founder Wally Liaw charged with conspiring to smuggle $2.5 billion in Nvidia GPU servers to China. Supermicro not named as defendant. Nvidia dropped 1.7%; Dell rose 6%.

Business

Markets

Third consecutive daily decline, fourth straight week in the red. S&P 500 fell 1.5% to 6,507, Nasdaq dropped 2.0% to 21,654, Dow shed 0.96% to 45,577. Russell 2000 in correction territory. Brent settled at $112.19/bbl (+3.3%), 10-year yield jumped to 4.38%, gold hit $4,667. Planet Labs surged 25% on breakeven Q4 and 41% revenue growth.

Canada Macro

Bank of Canada held at 2.25% Wednesday. CPI eased to 1.8% but BoC warned Middle East war will boost energy prices. Unemployment at 6.7%. Next: April 29.

US Macro

Fed held at 3.50–3.75%, 11-1 vote (Miran dissented). GDP growth revised to 0.9%, PCE raised to 2.7%. Job creation near zero, unemployment 4.4%. Seven of 19 FOMC officials expect no cuts this year.

Psalm of the Day — Psalm 1

1Blessed is the man who walks not in the counsel of the wicked, nor stands in the way of sinners, nor sits in the seat of scoffers; 2but his delight is in the law of the LORD, and on his law he meditates day and night. 3He is like a tree planted by streams of water that yields its fruit in its season, and its leaf does not wither. In all that he does, he prospers. 4The wicked are not so, but are like chaff that the wind drives away. 5Therefore the wicked will not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous; 6for the LORD knows the way of the righteous, but the way of the wicked will perish.

Scripture quotations are from the ESV® Bible (The Holy Bible, English Standard Version®), © 2001 by Crossway, a publishing ministry of Good News Publishers. Used by permission. All rights reserved.

Exegesis

Psalm 1 is a Torah psalm — a wisdom composition placed at the gateway of the Psalter to frame everything that follows as meditation on divine instruction. The architecture is starkly binary: two ways, two images. The righteous are a tree (ʿēṣ), planted and fruitful; the wicked are chaff (môṣ), weightless and wind-scattered. The opening ʾašrê is a declaration, not a prayer. The three verbs — walks, stands, sits — trace descent from casual association to settled identification. The Hebrew tôrâ carries the broader sense of "instruction." The tree image draws on ANE royal iconography but democratizes it. The verb šātal ("planted") implies deliberate placement by another agent. The closing yādaʿ ("knows") operates in its covenantal register: intimate, committed relationship. The psalm presents no third way.

SEP — Formal Epistemology

plato.stanford.edu/entries/formal-epistemology/

Formal epistemology applies mathematical tools — probability theory, decision theory, modal logic — to traditional epistemological questions. Bayesian epistemology models belief as degrees of credence and updates via conditionalization. Accuracy-first epistemology (Joyce, Pettigrew) derives probabilism from the norm that credences should minimize distance from truth. The field also covers belief revision (AGM framework), epistemic logic, and epistemic game theory. Critics argue formalization purchases precision at the cost of depth; defenders reply that informal epistemology has spent centuries circling the same problems. The field embodies "the hope that reason can be productively turned upon itself."

Cognitive Bias — Anchoring

Anchoring is the tendency to rely too heavily on the first piece of information you encounter, even when it is arbitrary. Tversky and Kahneman demonstrated this with a rigged wheel of fortune — those who saw 65 estimated higher than those who saw 10, despite the number being random. The same mechanism operates in salary negotiations, real estate, and courtroom sentencing. Expertise provides no immunity. The corrective: generate your own independent estimate before responding to any initial figure.
